prose:
The Fukui reactivity analysis of aniline was performed using Density Functional Theory (DFT) at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level of theory (tier 'standard') in the gas phase. The calculation was carried out on the geometry provided by the user, assuming a neutral molecule with charge 0 and spin multiplicity 1. The Fukui indices, which describe the susceptibility of different atomic sites to nucleophilic or electrophilic attack, were computed using a finite difference approach on Mulliken charges. The calculation was performed with the PySCF backend.

The results indicate the following:
*   The site most susceptible to **electrophilic attack** (the most nucleophilic site) is the **Nitrogen atom (N1)**, with a Fukui index for nucleophilic attack (`f-`) of **0.20452**. This site is most likely to act as a Lewis base and attack an electrophile.
*   The site most susceptible to **nucleophilic attack** (the most electrophilic site) is one of the ortho **Carbon atoms (C3)**, with a Fukui index for electrophilic attack (`f+`) of **2.2976**. This site is the most likely to be attacked by a nucleophile.

The integrity checks for the calculation were successful, and the sum of the Fukui indices was conserved, indicating a reliable result. No warnings were reported by the tool.

A summary of the computed Fukui indices for all atoms is below:

| Atom | Symbol | `f-` (Nucleophilic Attack) | `f+` (Electrophilic Attack) | `f0` (Radical Attack) |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| 1    | N      | 0.20452                      | 1.66966                       | 0.93709                 |
| 2    | C      | -0.00373                     | -1.90092                      | -0.95232                |
| 3    | C      | 0.06657                      | 2.29759                       | 1.18208                 |
| 4    | C      | 0.06659                      | 2.29656                       | 1.18158                 |
| 5    | C      | 0.00835                      | -0.28837                      | -0.14001                |
| 6    | C      | 0.00838                      | -0.28785                      | -0.13973                |
| 7    | C      | 0.10973                      | 1.40317                       | 0.75645                 |
| 8    | H      | 0.08200                      | -0.35139                      | -0.13470                |
| 9    | H      | 0.08201                      | -0.35134                      | -0.13466                |
| 10   | H      | 0.07635                      | -0.82107                      | -0.37236                |
| 11   | H      | 0.07635                      | -0.82131                      | -0.37248                |
| 12   | H      | 0.08737                      | -0.34269                      | -0.12766                |
| 13   | H      | 0.06776                      | -0.75088                      | -0.34156                |
| 14   | H      | 0.06775                      | -0.75116                      | -0.34171                |
